zh.lifeSummary: 當John Le Boef Giffard II於1275年1月19日出生在英國巴金哈姆爾特易福德時，他的父親John Giffard43歲，母親Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury43歲。他於大約1300年與Alexandra de Gardenis結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1328年3月30日在他的家鄉享年53歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾萊可哈普頓聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Le Boef Giffard II于1275年1月19日出生在英国巴金哈姆爾特易福德时，他的父亲John Giffard43岁，母亲Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury43岁。他于大约1300年与Alexandra de Gardenis结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1328年3月30日在他的家乡享年53岁，埋葬在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾萊可哈普頓聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Giffard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: (i) relationship name from the Continental Germanic personal name Gifard. (ii) nickname, perhaps sometimes from Old French giffard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Guiver
meanings: English: (i) nickname, apparently from Middle English giver ‘giver, donor, bestower’. (ii) variant of Giffard with voicing of /f/ to /v/ and loss of final -d.

variants.name: Gafford
meanings: English (southeastern): assimilated form of Gatford, a name of various origins. In Sussex and Surrey it is perhaps a variant of the habitational name Catford from a place so called in Somerset. Gatford is also from the habitational names Gateford (in Worksop, Nottinghamshire) or Gateforth (near Selby, Yorkshire), both formed with Old English gāt ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’, influenced by Old Norse geit ‘goat’.

Probably also an altered form of German Gaffert: habitational name from Gaffert near Köslin, Brandenburg, or from a personal name formed with Middle High German gate ‘fellow, companion’. The surname Gaffert is very rare in Germany.

English: perhaps an altered form of Gatewood , an occupational name for a ‘goatherd’ (Middle English gate-werde, Old English gātweard) or for a ‘gate keeper’ (Middle English gateward, Old English ʒeatweard, dialect gātward).

variants.name: Gifford
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Lewis   or respelling of   Lou  , as borne by the impresario  Lew Grade  (  1906–98 , originally  Louis Winogradsky ).

nameMeanings.name: Boef
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gabrielle, Henri.

English (of Norman origin): from Old French boeuf ‘bull, ox’, a nickname for a powerfully built man. In some cases it may have been originally a metonymic occupational name for a herdsman. Compare Bouvier .
German (Böff): from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with bod- (Old Saxon bodo ‘messenger’), as in Bodo .

variants.name: Bouvier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurier, Pascal, Adrien, Armand, Christophe, Constant, Dominique, Edouard, Jean Pierre, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Marcel.
French: occupational name for a herdsman, Old French bouvier (from Late Latin bovarius, a derivative of bos, genitive bovis ‘ox’). Compare Bouvia and Bovia .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When John Le Boef Giffard II was born on 19 January 1275, in Twyford, Buckinghamshire, England, his father, John Giffard, was 43 and his mother, Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury, was 43. He married Alexandra de Gardenis about 1300. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 30 March 1328, in his hometown, at the age of 53, and was buried in St Peter's Church, Leckhampton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Le Boef Giffard II est né le 19 janvier 1275, à Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John Giffard, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury, avait 43 ans. Il a épousé Alexandra de Gardenis environ 1300. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 30 mars 1328, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à St Peter's Church, Leckhampton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Januar 1275 in Twyford, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Giffard 43 und seine Mutter Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury 43. Er hat um 1300 Alexandra de Gardenis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 30. März 1328 im Alter von 53 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in St Peter's Church, Leckhampton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: John Le Boef Giffard II(이)가 1275년 1월 19일 영국 벅잉함실르 트w이퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Giffard(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury(은)는 43세였다. 그는 대략 1300년 Alexandra de Gardenis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1328년 3월 30일 그의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 렉함프턴 스트 피터'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Le Boef Giffard II nasceu em 19 de janeiro de 1275, em Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Giffard, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury, tinha 43 anos. Ele casou-se com Alexandra de Gardenis aproximadamente 1300. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Le Boef Giffard II родился 19 января 1275 г. в местности твифорд, буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Giffard было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury, 43. Он женился на женщине по имени Alexandra de Gardenis около 1300 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 30 марта 1328 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности ст питир'с чурч, леккхамптон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
